> [!WARNING] > ## Rate limit exceeded > > `@Techlemariam` has exceeded the limit for the number of commits that can be reviewed per hour. Please wait **1 minutes and 49 seconds** before requesting another review. > >
> ⌛ How to resolve this issue? > > After the wait time has elapsed, a review can be triggered using the `@coderabbitai review` command as a PR comment. Alternatively, push new commits to this PR. > > We recommend that you space out your commits to avoid hitting the rate limit. > >
> > >
> 🚦 How do rate limits work? > > CodeRabbit enforces hourly rate limits for each developer per organization. > > Our paid plans have higher rate limits than the trial, open-source and free plans. In all cases, we re-allow further reviews after a brief timeout. > > Please see our [FAQ](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/faq) for further information. > >
> >
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough Introducerar ett nytt "Factory" multi-station-system: PowerShell-orkestrerare och GitHub Actions för stationer (design, fabrication, verify, ship), backend Prisma-modell och serveraction för fabriksstatus, frontend Next.js dashboard med statuskort, plus uppdaterade arbetsflöden och dokumentation (version 2.0.0). ## Changes |Cohort / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Orkestrering & CI**
` .agent/scripts/factory-manager.ps1`, `.github/workflows/factory-line.yml` | Lägger till PowerShell-orkestrerare som hanterar stationer (design, fabrication, verify, ship) och en GitHub Actions-workflow som triggar skriptet (manuellt eller vid push till `specs/**`).| |**Arbetsflöden (version 2.0.0)**
`.agent/workflows/...` (`analyst.md`, `architect.md`, `coder.md`, `deploy.md`, `polish.md`, `qa.md`, `security.md`, `ui-ux.md`, `factory.md`, `domain-session.md`) | Uppgraderar flera workflow-dokument till "Level 10" och omskriver struktur till "Core Philosophy", "Toolbelt" och "Factory Protocol" med stationsbaserad process; lägger till ny `factory.md` och ändrar session-mappning för Factory Run.| |**Datamodell & serverlogik**
`prisma/schema.prisma`, `src/actions/factory.ts`, `.storybook/prisma-mock.ts` | Ny Prisma-modell `FactoryStatus`; serveraction `getFactoryStatus()` med seed-logik; mock-typ för Storybook.| |**Frontend: Dashboard**
`src/app/factory/page.tsx`, `src/components/factory/StatusGrid.tsx`, `src/components/factory/StatusCard.tsx`, `specs/pilot-factory-dashboard.md` | Ny Next.js-sida för `/factory`, komponenter StatusGrid/StatusCard för att rendera stationshälsa, och en specs-fil som beskriver dashboardens användarhistorier, design och tester.| |**Tester**
`tests/unit/factory.test.ts` | Enhetstester för `getFactoryStatus()` som mockar Prisma och verifierar seed- och fetch-beteende.| |**Konfiguration & integrationslista**
`.gemini/settings.json` | Utökad MCP-serverkonfiguration med flera nya tjänster och uppdaterad token-hantering.| ## Sequence Diagram ```mermaid sequenceDiagram actor User participant GitHub as GitHub Actions participant CLI as PowerShell\nfactory-manager.ps1 participant API as Server Action\ngetFactoryStatus() participant DB as Prisma\nFactoryStatus participant UI as React Dashboard\nStatusGrid User->>GitHub: Trigger factory-line workflow\n(manual eller push to specs/) GitHub->>CLI: Kör factory-manager.ps1\n(Feature, Station) alt Design Station CLI->>CLI: Validera spec-fil\n(kontrollerar required sections) CLI-->>GitHub: Logga success/error else Fabrication Station CLI->>CLI: Initierar fabrication\n(placeholder AI-invokation) else Verify Station CLI->>CLI: Startar QC\n(kör gatekeeper eller npm test) else Ship Station CLI->>CLI: Förbereder shipment\n(hint: PR-creation) end User->>UI: Besök /factory dashboard UI->>API: Hämta factory status API->>DB: findMany(FactoryStatus) alt Ingen data API->>DB: Seed default stations API->>DB: findMany() igen end DB-->>API: Returnera status[] API-->>UI: FactoryStatusData[] UI->>UI: Render StatusGrid\nmed StatusCard:ar UI-->>User: Visualisera station health ``` ## Uppskattad kodgranskningsansträngning 🎯 4 (Komplex) | ⏱️ ~45 minuter ## Dikt > 🐰 *I verkstadens lugn jag hoppar lätt,* > *från spec till kod, en stig så rätt.* > *Fem stationer sjunger, hälsa i rad,* > *vi seedar, testar — allt blir glatt.* > *Nivå tio nu, vi jobbar med bravur och skratt.*
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 2 | ❌ 2
❌ Failed checks (2 warnings) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 0.00% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. | | Merge Conflict Detection | ⚠️ Warning | ❌ Merge conflicts detected (13 files):

⚔️ `.agent/workflows/analyst.md` (content)
⚔️ `.agent/workflows/architect.md` (content)
⚔️ `.agent/workflows/coder.md` (content)
⚔️ `.agent/workflows/deploy.md` (content)

⚔️ `prisma/schema.prisma` (content)

These conflicts must be resolved before merging into `main`. | Resolve conflicts locally and push changes to this branch. |
✅ Passed checks (2 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :---------------: | :------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Description Check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped - CodeRabbit’s high-level summary is enabled. | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The pull request title clearly summarizes the main change: introducing a pilot factory dashboard feature. It directly corresponds to the primary deliverable in the changeset. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing touches
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Post copyable unit tests in a comment - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feat/pilot-factory-dashboard`

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 17**
🤖 Fix all issues with AI agents ``` In @.agent/scripts/factory-manager.ps1: - Around line 1-4: Validate the $Feature parameter immediately after the param block: reject or sanitize any value containing path traversal sequences (../ or ..\), directory separators, or characters outside an allowed pattern (e.g. only letters, numbers, underscores and hyphens). If validation fails, write an error and exit; otherwise use the validated value when constructing "specs/$Feature.md". Reference the param variables $Feature and $Station and the path "specs/$Feature.md" when adding this check. - Around line 75-77: The comment header "# Station 6: Shipping (`@delivery`)" is incorrect; change the comment to read "# Station 5: Shipping (`@delivery`)" so it matches the station numbering in .agent/workflows/factory.md; update the string in the header comment line inside the .agent/scripts/factory-manager.ps1 script where "# Station 6: Shipping (`@delivery`)" appears. - Around line 64-72: Replace the unsafe Invoke-Expression $Gatekeeper with the call operator (& $Gatekeeper) to execute the gatekeeper script, and after the fallback commands npm run build and npm run test check their exit status (using $LASTEXITCODE or $? ) and fail/exit if non-zero so build/test failures are propagated; update references in this block where $Gatekeeper and the fallback npm commands appear. In @.agent/workflows/architect.md: - Around line 40-46: The documentation references two non-existent PowerShell scripts; update the workflow to either add the missing scripts or point to the real files: create and commit .agent/skills/nextjs-route-visualizer/scripts/visualize.ps1 (or adjust the path to the actual visualizer script), and either implement .agent/skills/prisma-migrator/scripts/check-drift.ps1 or change the docs to call the existing .agent/skills/prisma-migrator/scripts/migrate.ps1 (and verify the exact script names/paths in both .agent/skills/* directories before merging). - Line 69: Arbetsflödet i .agent/workflows/architect.md refererar till implementation_plan.md som saknas; antingen skapa filen implementation_plan.md i projektroten eller ändra referensen i Architect-agentens konfiguration i .agent/workflows/architect.md så att den pekar på den korrekta, existerande filvägen/namnet; uppdatera även eventuella variabler eller steg i Architect-agenten som använder "implementation_plan.md" så att sökväg/filnamn stämmer överens. In @.agent/workflows/factory.md: - Line 96: Det finns en felaktig checkbox-syntax i raden "-[ ] [High] [Feature] shortcut description "; ändra "-[ ]" till "- [ ]" så att GitHub Markdown renderar en kryssruta korrekt, behåll resten av raden oförändrad så att taggarna "[High] [Feature]" och kommentaren "" stannar kvar. In @.gemini/settings.json: - Around line 34-36: The current settings.json sets NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED = "0", which disables TLS validation; replace this by removing that env entry and instead document and implement a safe alternative: set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED only in a local/dev-only configuration with a clear comment, or better yet add the internal CA certificate to the Node trust store (e.g., via NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS or platform trust store) and remove NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED; update the settings.json entry for "env" (the NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED symbol) and add a short rationale comment explaining why any dev-only override exists and how to use the internal CA instead. - Around line 55-62: The current postgres task passes the connection string via the args array ("${env:DIRECT_URL}") which makes it visible in process lists; instead, modify the "postgres" config so the CLI arg uses a non-global env variable (e.g., "${DATABASE_URL}") and add an "env" block that assigns DATABASE_URL from the original ${env:DIRECT_URL} so the secret is not stored as a system-wide environment variable; update the "postgres" object and its "args" and "env" entries and keep the call to "@modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres" unchanged. In @.github/workflows/factory-line.yml: - Around line 20-24: Byt ut ogiltiga permissions-nyckeln "projects" mot den korrekta "repository-projects" i permissions-blocket (behåll värdet, t.ex. write) så att GitHub Actions accepterar den; uppdatera permissions-listan där "projects: write" förekommer och kör actionlint för att verifiera att inga andra ogiltiga nycklar finns kvar. - Around line 43-51: Add a guard around the git diff call to handle cases where github.event.before is unset or the all-zero SHA (initial/force push): check the github.event.before value used when setting $ChangedFiles and if it is null/empty or matches an all-zero SHA, avoid running git diff with those values and instead obtain changed files from the target commit (${{ github.event.after }}) using a safe fallback such as git show --name-only ${{ github.event.after }} or git ls-tree -r --name-only ${{ github.event.after }}; update the logic that sets $ChangedFiles and keeps the downstream $SpecFile/$Feature extraction the same so detection still works on initial pushes. - Around line 39-41: Remove the unused OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable from the workflow's env block to avoid exposing an unnecessary secret; specifically, delete the OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }} entry under env, verify there are no references to OPENAI_API_KEY in scripts such as factory-manager.ps1 or other CI steps (search for "OPENAI_API_KEY") and only re-add it conditionally if a workflow step actually requires it, leaving GH_TOKEN intact. - Around line 34-35: The workflow step currently named "Install Dependencies" runs npm ci which conflicts with package.json's declared packageManager pnpm@9.15.0 and the pnpm-lock.yaml; update that step to use pnpm instead—either replace the command with "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile" or add the official pnpm setup-action then run "pnpm install --frozen-lockfile" so the workflow consistently uses pnpm (locate the step labeled Install Dependencies in the workflow). In `@prisma/schema.prisma`: - Around line 1149-1155: The FactoryStatus Prisma model allows duplicate station names; add a uniqueness constraint to the station field by updating the model so station has `@unique` to prevent duplicate rows during concurrent seeding; update the Prisma schema's FactoryStatus model (the station field) and run prisma migrate/dev or apply the migration so getFactoryStatus in src/actions/factory.ts (which seeds 5 stations) cannot create duplicates under race conditions. In `@specs/pilot-factory-dashboard.md`: - Around line 69-78: Add role-based authorization and input validation: implement a Zod schema named FactoryStatusSchema (or FactoryStatus) and use it to validate any incoming payloads in src/actions/factory.ts, then enforce ROLE_ADMIN in the server-side handler(s) (e.g., the function that fetches/returns factory status in src/actions/factory.ts) by checking the authenticated user's roles and returning/throwing a 403 if not present; also ensure server-side rendering in src/app/factory/page.tsx only calls the status handler for admins (perform the same role check or call the authorized server function) so the page doesn't expose data to non-admins. In `@src/actions/factory.ts`: - Around line 14-31: getFactoryStatus() can trigger seedFactoryStatus() concurrently causing duplicate stations; add a unique constraint on FactoryStatus.station in the Prisma schema and change the seeding to use createMany(..., { skipDuplicates: true }) or perform upsert logic so inserts are idempotent; update seedFactoryStatus() to either loop with prisma.factoryStatus.upsert(...) for each station or call prisma.factoryStatus.createMany({ data: [...], skipDuplicates: true }) and leave getFactoryStatus() unchanged (it can still call seedFactoryStatus() on empty result). In `@src/components/factory/StatusCard.tsx`: - Around line 44-50: Add proper ARIA support and clamp the health value: ensure the progress container element rendered in StatusCard.tsx has role="progressbar" and includes aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin="0" and aria-valuemax="100", and set aria-valuenow to a sanitized value; compute a clampedHealth (e.g., Math.min(100, Math.max(0, status.health || 0))) and use clampedHealth both for the inline width style (`width: `${clampedHealth}%``) and for aria-valuenow; keep the existing isError logic for coloring but base width/aria on clampedHealth to prevent negative or >100 widths. - Around line 23-26: The status indicator div in StatusCard.tsx currently conveys state only via color; update the element that uses isError/isBusy (the pulsing
) to include an accessible label by adding an aria-label (e.g., "Error", "Busy", "Available" based on isError/isBusy) or render a visually hidden text node that announces the same state; ensure the label text changes with the same logic that sets the background class so screen readers receive the correct current status. ```
🧹 Nitpick comments (8)
tests/unit/factory.test.ts (2)
`20-50`: **Överväg att lägga till ett test för felhanteringsfallet.** `getFactoryStatus` i `src/actions/factory.ts` har en `try/catch` som returnerar `[]` vid fel. Det saknas ett test som verifierar detta beteende — t.ex. när `findMany` kastar ett undantag.
🧪 Förslag på felhanteringstest ```diff + it('should return empty array on error', async () => { + (prisma.factoryStatus.findMany as any).mockRejectedValue(new Error('DB down')); + + const result = await getFactoryStatus(); + + expect(result).toEqual([]); + }); ```
--- `35-50`: **Testet verifierar inte det returnerade värdet vid seeding.** Rad 44 anropar `await getFactoryStatus()` men resultatet sparas inte och inget assert görs på returvärdet. Det vore bra att verifiera att seedad data faktiskt returneras.
🧪 Förslag ```diff - await getFactoryStatus(); + const result = await getFactoryStatus(); + + expect(result).toHaveLength(1); + expect(result[0].station).toBe('design'); ```
src/app/factory/page.tsx (2)
`6-24`: **Tomt tillstånd hanteras inte när `getFactoryStatus` returnerar en tom array.** Om `getFactoryStatus` returnerar `[]` (t.ex. vid databasfel) visas bara en tom container utan feedback till användaren. Överväg att lägga till ett tomt tillståndsmeddelande.
💡 Förslag på empty state ```diff
- + {statusData.length > 0 ? ( + + ) : ( +

+ No factory status data available. +

+ )}
```
--- `6-7`: **`/factory`-routen är redan skyddad av middleware – lägg till explicit auth-kontroll i `getFactoryStatus` för konsekvens.** Routen är redan skyddad av middleware som omdirigerar oautentiserade användare till `/login`. Dock saknar `getFactoryStatus` i `src/actions/factory.ts` en explicit autentiseringskontroll, vilket avviker från projektets övergripande mönster. Alla andra server actions utför `getUser()`-kontroller. För kodkonsistens bör en explicit auth-guard läggas till i funktionen.
src/components/factory/StatusCard.tsx (1)
`8-8`: **`isHealthy` är deklarerad men aldrig använd** Statisk analys (CodeFactor) bekräftar att `isHealthy` aldrig refereras. Ta bort den eller använd den i renderingslogiken för att undvika dead code.
♻️ Föreslagen fix ```diff export function StatusCard({ status }: Props) { - const isHealthy = status.health > 80; const isBusy = !!status.current; const isError = status.health < 50; ```
src/actions/factory.ts (2)
`4-4`: **Oanvänd import: `revalidatePath`** CodeFactor bekräftar att `revalidatePath` aldrig används. Ta bort den.
🧹 Föreslagen fix ```diff 'use server'; import { prisma } from '@/lib/prisma'; -import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; ```
--- `27-30`: **Fel sväljs tyst — UI:t kan inte skilja "tomt" från "trasigt"** `catch`-blocket returnerar `[]` utan att signalera felet uppåt. Dashboarden kommer att visa ett tomt tillstånd utan indikation på att något gick fel. Överväg att antingen kasta om felet, returnera ett resultatobjekt med felstatus, eller logga på ett sätt som når operatörerna (inte bara `console.error`).
.agent/workflows/architect.md (1)
`54-54`: **Förtydliga formuleringen om Prisma-schema.** Formuleringen "Must use invalid Prisma code? No, define the *changes* clearly." är förvirrande. Omformulera för att tydligt förklara att schema-ändringar ska definieras korrekt och valideras, inte som ogiltig kod.
📝 Föreslagen omformulering ```diff -1. **Schema**: Must use invalid Prisma code? No, define the *changes* clearly. +1. **Schema**: Define schema changes clearly using valid Prisma syntax. ```
